releases.summary: Anti-slop QA gate (GATE 8): every variant is self-audited against references/anti-slop.md before delivery — refusing AI-slop tropes (hero-metric, eyebrow-reflex, side-stripe cards, gradient text, three-tints-of-one-layout) while never breaking readable contrast or type hierarchy. Adapted for 1080×1350 from the Impeccable anti-pattern system.
notes: references/anti-slop.md = the checklist retranslated to social visuals + the style-pack exemption (an invoked Doodle/Paper pack is voice, not slop; never-exempt checks still hold).

tools/anti-slop/ vendors Impeccable's deterministic detector (detect-antipatterns.js) + critique flow under Apache-2.0; the 700KB live-browser tooling was left out. Optional machine pass; judgment calls stay with the agent.
Wired into the SKILL description as a 6th hard rule, the RUNBOOK as step 6, and a new 'Anti-Slop Gate' Principles card.

releases.summary: Unattended daily auto-update: a scheduled GitHub Action (~12:00) clones the private master repo and installs new versions automatically, honouring ownership (overrides/ + client/ untouched).
notes: New: .github/workflows/daily-update.yml (cron, runs on GitHub's servers whether or not the project is open).
New scripts: tools/check-update.cjs (exit 10 = behind) + tools/apply-update.cjs (installs master-owned files only).
Setup in tools/CONNECT.md: set MASTER_REPO + add DS_SYNC_TOKEN secret (read-only PAT). A static design system can't self-run a daemon — a scheduled Action is the only true unattended sync.

releases.summary: Single entry point: the app's sub-views (Visual Board, START HERE + their JS) moved into app/ so only LinkedIn Visual Designer.html is openable. Verified all three views still load.
notes: Moved files: app/Visual Board.html, app/visual-board.js, app/board-editor.js, app/START HERE.html.
Relative paths corrected (../styles.css, ../overrides, ../ui_kits, ../_ds_bundle.js); board-editor base-guesses gained a ../ depth.
App-shell iframe srcs + start-here template links repointed at app/.

releases.summary: Paper style pack (editorial, outline-only, paper-grain) + style packs reframed as composable, colour/font-agnostic skillsets the assistant reaches for and blends.
notes: Packs compose: composition packs (Bento = tile mosaic) layer with skin packs (Doodle, Paper) — e.g. Bento blocks in Doodle style.
Every pack is colour- & font-agnostic: it supplies the treatment, wears the brand layer (--brand-font, --brand-primary accent); named palette/font is a fallback, signature display font is optional.
Used proactively by the assistant, named by the user, or set as a base style. Root SKILL.md + style-packs/README.md rewritten.

releases.summary: Bento style pack — premium dark-first editorial: near-black canvas, asymmetric hairline-tile mosaic, oversized Inter-Tight type, mono metadata, one accent glow. Translated to the 1080×1350 archetypes.
notes: Tiles share the #0A0A0A canvas; 1px hairline borders (not fills) define them; clean 32px radius, flat, no tilt.
Brand colour folds in as the single accent (focal stat/word/CTA + soft glow on the focal tile only).
A pack restyles the look only — one board, 3 variants, analogy-led, safe margins, GATE 7 still win.

releases.summary: Style Packs — optional aesthetic layers switched on by name. First pack: Doodle (hand-drawn), translated from web-UI to the 1080×1350 LinkedIn archetypes.
notes: style-packs/<slug>/ = SKILL.md + a @dsCard group="Style Packs" card; users can drop in their own (skeuomorphic, neumorphic, bento).
Doodle: sketch borders, solid offset shadows, hand-lettered headline (Delius Swash Caps), cream canvas + dot-grid, doodle marks from the Icon Library, subtle tilt.
A pack restyles the look only — one board, 3 variants, analogy-led, safe margins, GATE 7 still win; brand colour folds in as the accent.

releases.summary: Board editor: the Icon Library is now a drag source — your drawn/imported icons appear in My library, ready to drag onto the canvas (recolour to brand).
notes: My library auto-surfaces IconKit marks, custom icons (localStorage 'icon-custom') and illustrations under 'From the Icon Library'.
Icons render inline so they recolour to --brand-primary/--icon-ink; dropped icons carry data-shape="icon" (resize keeps aspect).
Refreshes live on icon-changed / storage events; empty kit shows the graceful empty state.

releases.summary: Settings: editable profile (name · role · @handle · photo) feeds the live in-feed preview and the visual's @handle.
Settings rebuilt as ONE page with a live LinkedIn feed preview (left = all brand options, right = the post recolouring as you change settings). No more 5 steps.
App nav: replaced the heavy left rail with a small floating dock (icon-only), full-bleed views; reads as a tool, not an app shell.
One workspace: LinkedIn Visual Designer.html wraps Visual Board, Icon Library and Settings into a single app (each is the standalone file loaded as a pane; nothing renamed).
notes: Image Library stubbed as 'Soon'. Standalone files keep working; chat stays the primary surface.

releases.summary: Icon & illustration library added to the core (components/icons/): brand-agnostic hand-drawn engine + editor, draw/import (incl. SVG/Figma), recolours to the brand, ships empty.
notes: Web components <icon-mark>/<icon-ill> + IconKit.mark('…'); edits persist. New 'Icon Library' card.

releases.summary: Editor: the upload button now sits under the My library heading where it belongs.
Board '+ New visual' / 'Iterate' open a modal that prepares the full /linkedin-visual prompt (paste post / describe change) and copies it to hand to the assistant.
notes: The artifact can't auto-send into the chat (cross-origin) — copy-and-paste is the path; the AI still leads.

releases.summary: Board buttons: reliable copy in sandboxed previews (execCommand → async API → pre-selected fallback box).
Board buttons copy a ready-made /linkedin-visual prompt (rules baked in) instead of just nudging to chat — agent-first.
Always-on enforcement (the flow + five hard rules apply whenever connected and asked, no slash needed) and Visual Board exposed as a one-click Template.
notes: /linkedin-visual and /ds-setup remain explicit triggers.

releases.summary: Editor: multi-select (marquee + shift-click); the whole selection moves and Delete/Duplicate/Colour/Bring-to-front act on all at once.
Editor: drop new elements onto the pasteboard outside the canvas; the element toolbar tracks the selection everywhere.
Editor: off-canvas elements stay selectable (DOM containment); smoother edge-crossing (snap only inside the canvas); click empty stage clears selection.
Editor: small/zoomed-out elements are grabbable again — resize handles hide under ~44px on screen so the whole box is a move target.
Editor: pasteboard — park elements outside the 1080×1350 artboard while composing; off-canvas content is clipped on export and in thumbnails.
Editor: colour-swatch picker (the DS's real colours + custom, saved per board) and a magnetic 12-column grid with Grid (blue) / Margins (red unsafe zone) guides.
notes: Snapping always on; hold Alt/Option to place freely. State persists.

releases.summary: Editor: palette items carry icons; My library auto-detects the DS's own icon set via assets/library-manifest.json; UI chrome locked to Inter (only the canvas adopts --brand-font).
Hard rule GATE 7: fill the canvas — content on every frame/slide/panel must be vertically balanced, never top-pinned leaving the bottom half empty.
Slash-command reliability: both commands front-loaded into the SKILL description so they fire even when the body isn't weighed heavily; a pasted post is not build permission.
Slash-command triggers: /ds-setup opens the START HERE brand wizard; /linkedin-visual enters the full RUNBOOK with all five hard rules enforced (questions first, never build immediately).

One unified, numbered template library: merged the 'Reference ·' and 'Bold ·' sets (removed 15 overlaps), renamed everything to templates/<type>-NN-<slug>/ (single-01…48, quote-01…04, infographic-01…06, carousel-01…09).
notes: Carousels are explicitly free to use ANY archetype per slide. GATE 6 rewired to the new scheme + references/method.md as the catalog.

releases.summary: Bold Visual Method + 65 named archetype templates (theme-agnostic .dc.html, recolour live from the brand layer) + a content layer (overrides/voice.md, icp.md, offer.md). Templates picker → 83.
notes: The Bold Visual Method shipped as a Principles card + references/method.md, wired into SKILL.md GATE 6 as the build philosophy.

releases.summary: Archetype template library (9 reference templates × A/B/C variants incl. carousels-on-board), a Canva-style board editor, and winning-variant selection.
notes: Carousels live on the Visual Board as a scrollable filmstrip; reference templates are SLIDE-CONTENT ONLY.
Board editor: select/move/resize/edit-text, library rail, duplicate/recolour, zoom, per-variant persistence.
GATE 5 (analogy-led) + GATE 6 (rotate archetypes) added; removed the Brief Studio and first-run setup kits.
START HERE is now a guided 5-step brand wizard with one-click 'Copy for the assistant'.

releases.summary: Visual Board (one Canva-style board per project, per-visual PNG/HTML export) plus two hard behaviour gates: ask-before-build, and all-visuals-on-one-board.
notes: GATE 1: the assistant asks questions and offers directions before building any visual.
GATE 2: every visual is a block on one Visual Board — never a file per visual.
Setup screen fixes: starts at step 1, live preview reflects font/colour, input focus retained.

releases.summary: Foundation release — the colour- & font-agnostic system, four templates, two kits, and the master/branch model.
notes: 24px hard safe band on all visuals.
Brief Studio (intake + live feed preview) and Visual Library (export PNG/HTML + approve-to-learn).
Master → branch model: overrides/ + client/ are never overwritten.
